title: Sentry MCP via uvx with an --auth-token CLI arg → REVIEW (unpinned), token-as-arg is not a secret env

origin.description: Public documentation config from getsentry/sentry-mcp (README.md, uvx/stdio variant). Verbatim except normalization to a valid JSON root. The --auth-token value YOUR_SENTRY_TOKEN is upstream's own placeholder.

humanExpectation.rationale: An unpinned uvx package (mcp-server-sentry, no version) → supply.unpinned-package (high, S1) → REVIEW. The credential is passed as a CLI argument (--auth-token YOUR_SENTRY_TOKEN), NOT as an env var, so secrets.env-key (which keys on env-key NAMES) correctly does NOT fire — a true-negative this case pins. exec.unverified-local-source also does not fire because uvx is a recognized package runner with a parsed package name.
knownLimitations: secrets.env-key reads env-key names, not argv. A token passed as a CLI arg value (--auth-token <v>) is not surfaced as a secret here — CallLint flags env-shaped credential keys, not argument values. The token risk is real but lives in a surface CallLint deliberately does not parse for secrets.
